text: Wrist Articulation II is a much more difficult and practical version of the wrist articulation exercises.
The visual field displays multiple spheres encased within glass globes, each globe featuring openings positioned at specific angles.
The instrument is maneuvered to touch the sphere inside the glass globe, avoiding contact with the sides of the globe and without applying pressure to the glass.

Camera utilization is important in this exercise as to provide the appropriate view of the opening in the glass globe and thus allows you to minimize penalties in regards to touching the globe or applying unnecessary pressure.

The operator uses hand controls to straighten the instrument tips as they enter through the glass opening, successfully touching the inner sphere while avoiding contact with the glass edges and external pressure on the globe.
Take your time as you pass your instrument through the glass opening to maximize your accuracy.
And avoid hitting the glass spheres as you move your instruments around the field.
Success in this exercise requires good camera coordination, two-arm coordination, and maximizing the degrees of freedom with wrist articulation.
